title: Freedom Score
subtitle: Data by [Freedom House](#dod:freedom-house). The score captures the extent of free and fair elections, political pluralism and participation, a functioning government, freedom of expression and association, the rule of law, and personal autonomy. Higher scores indicate more freedom.
caption:
Freedom Score, 2025
Data by Freedom House. The score captures the extent of free and fair elections, political pluralism and participation, a functioning government, freedom of expression and association, the rule of law, and personal autonomy. Higher scores indicate more freedom.

This choropleth map displays the 2025 Freedom Score for countries worldwide, using a continuous color scale ranging from pale yellow (20) to dark blue (101) to represent the extent of civil liberties and political rights. The highest values, falling in the darkest blue band between 80 and 101, appear across North America, including the United States and Canada, as well as Western Europe, Australia, and Japan. South America shows a mix of mid-range and high scores, with countries like Argentina and Brazil appearing in the medium blue bands. In contrast, the lowest scores, represented by pale yellow and light green shades between 20 and 40, dominate the map across North Africa, the Middle East, and Central Asia. Russia and China sit in the lowest value bands, indicating restricted political participation and civil liberties according to the metric. India and Indonesia appear in the middle of the scale, colored in teal shades that correspond to the 40 to 60 range. The map includes a "No data" category marked by diagonal hatching, visible over Greenland and a few other territories. The score captures the extent of free and fair elections, political pluralism and participation, a functioning government, freedom of expression and association, the rule of law, and personal autonomy, with higher scores indicating more freedom. The geographic distribution shows a clear concentration of high scores in the Americas and Western Europe, while low scores cluster across Africa and Asia.
